chapter: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: What is time?
questions.answer: Time is the measure of duration between two events.

questions.question: What is motion?
questions.answer: Motion is the change in position of an object with respect to time.

questions.question: Give two examples of objects in motion.
questions.answer: A moving car and a flying bird are examples of objects in motion.

questions.question: What instrument is used to measure time?
questions.answer: Clocks and stopwatches are instruments used to measure time.

questions.question: What is a stopwatch?
questions.answer: A stopwatch is a device used to measure short intervals of time accurately.

questions.question: Define periodic motion.
questions.answer: Periodic motion is motion that repeats itself at equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Give an example of periodic motion.
questions.answer: The swinging of a pendulum is an example of periodic motion.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of time?
questions.answer: The SI unit of time is the second.

questions.question: How was time measured in ancient days?
questions.answer: In ancient times, people used the movement of the Sun, water clocks, and sand clocks to measure time.

questions.question: What is a sundial?
questions.answer: A sundial is a device that tells time using the shadow cast by the Sun.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.answer: Speed is the distance travelled by an object in a unit of time.

questions.question: Write the formula for speed.
questions.answer: Speed = Distance ÷ Time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of speed?
questions.answer: The SI unit of speed is metre per second (m/s).

questions.question: Name two units used to measure time.
questions.answer: Second and minute are two units used to measure time.

questions.question: What is uniform motion?
questions.answer: Uniform motion is when an object covers equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is non-uniform motion?
questions.answer: Non-uniform motion is when an object covers unequal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Why is accurate time measurement important?
questions.answer: Accurate time measurement helps in calculating speed, scheduling activities, and studying motion.

questions.question: What is a pendulum?
questions.answer: A pendulum is a weight suspended from a fixed point that swings back and forth.

questions.question: How does a pendulum help measure time?
questions.answer: The regular swinging motion of a pendulum can be used to measure equal intervals of time.

questions.question: What is a time interval?
questions.answer: A time interval is the duration between two events.

questions.question: Name two modern devices used to measure time accurately.
questions.answer: Digital clocks and atomic clocks are modern devices used for accurate time measurement.

questions.question: What is distance?
questions.answer: Distance is the total length of the path travelled by an object.

questions.question: How are time and motion related?
questions.answer: Motion describes how an object's position changes with respect to time.

questions.question: Why were sundials not always reliable?
questions.answer: Sundials do not work at night or during cloudy weather because they depend on sunlight.

questions.question: Give one example of motion in daily life.
questions.answer: A bus moving on the road is an example of motion in daily life.
